mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows, directly increasing present valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns during rate cuts are not significantly higher than baseline periods.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate exceeding 5.0 percent signals labor market deterioration that reduces consumer spending power and corporate profit expectations, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the following week.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in more than 65 percent of instances where UNRATE exceeds 5.0 percent, or the signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.02) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real GDP and retail sales indicates broad-based economic strength that boosts corporate earnings growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive 60-day returns in over 55 percent of triggered observations, or the interaction term shows no statistical significance in multivariate regression models.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indices reflect expanding manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which directly supports corporate revenue growth and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank correlation between INDPRO momentum and 20-day forward returns remains below 0.04 across all rolling 3-year windows, or the signal reverses direction during high volatility periods.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price index values above 215.0 indicate accelerating inflation that forces central banks to tighten policy, raising borrowing costs and compressing equity valuations to produce negative S&P 500 forward returns over the following week.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns remain positive in more than 60 percent of months where CPIAUCSL exceeds 215.0, or the inflation threshold shows no predictive edge in out-of-sample walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Weak nonfarm payrolls growth combined with rising unemployment reveals structural labor market weakness that suppresses wage growth and consumer demand, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal predicts negative 60-day returns in fewer than 40 percent of cases, or the interaction effect vanishes when controlling for monetary policy shocks.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts exceeding 1.0 million units signal strong residential investment and construction activity, which drives upstream supply chain demand and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX delivers negative 20-day returns in more than 55 percent of months where HOUST surpasses 1000.0, or the housing threshold loses predictive accuracy during rate hike cycles.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production alongside rising consumer prices indicates supply-side bottlenecks that erode profit margins and trigger risk-off sentiment, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the following week.
failure_condition: The stagflation signal fails to predict negative 5-day returns in over 60 percent of triggered instances, or the joint effect shows no incremental explanatory power over single-series models.
